toolUseResult.originalFile:
# homelab-iac

Ansible-driven, git-versioned deployment of the netns-VPN-jail homelab
(thurgadin / convoker / svc-download / svc-media). This is the automation
layer over the `homelab-vpn-bundle` design — same architecture, same gates,
now runnable as one command with a check-back-later ntfy ping.

Run everything from the MBP. It talks to the PVE **API** (token) for VM
lifecycle and **SSH** for in-guest config. cloud-init stays minimal (identity
+ packages); Ansible owns everything after first boot, so day-two changes and
drift checks use the same code.

## What is and isn't automated

| Phase (DEPLOY.md)            | Here?  | Why |
|------------------------------|--------|-----|
| 1 TrueNAS storage            | **No** | One-time; the encryption reboot test you *want* to witness. Do it by hand. |
| 2 PVE host prep (RAM, order) | Partly | Snippet content + boot order are one-liners in DEPLOY; do those once. |
| 3/5 VM creation              | Yes    | `pve_vm` role via the API |
| 4 Jail + stack               | Yes    | `svc_download` role, gates as assertions |
| 6 Media stack                | Yes    | `svc_media` role, incl. Tailscale + Caddy **when `vault_tailscale_authkey` is set** (skipped with a visible note otherwise) |
| 7 App wiring                 | No     | Clicking through SAB/Prowlarr/arr/Jellyseerr UIs |
| 9 Backups                    | Yes    | Nightly timers on both VMs: RomM DB dump + appdata tars (media, 03:05), arr/SAB appdata tars (download, 03:10), 14-day retention. ABS uses its built-in backup to the NFS-mounted `/config/backups` — schedule it once in the UI. **Restore is manual; test it quarterly.** |
| 9 ntfy server                | No     | Stand it up on svc-media once; until then every push prints a loud WARNING in play output (or point `ntfy_url` at ntfy.sh/<random-topic> to bootstrap) |

Mullvad key generation is manual by nature.

## One-time setup

```bash
# 0. control node deps (MBP) — a project venv, because Homebrew's python
#    refuses global pip installs (PEP 668). The Makefile auto-detects .venv.
python3 -m venv .venv
.venv/bin/pip install ansible "proxmoxer>=2.0" requests
.venv/bin/ansible-galaxy collection install -r requirements.yml

# 1. PVE API token (on thurgadin, once) — SCOPED in v5: pool + two storages,
#    privilege separation stays ON. Full command block with rationale lives in
#    inventory/group_vars/all_vault.yml.example; short version:
pveum pool add homelab-svc --comment "svc-download + svc-media"
pveum user add automation@pve
pveum user token add automation@pve ansible                # copy the secret
set +H   # MUST be its own line first: interactive bash history expansion
         # mangles the !ansible token id ("event not found") otherwise
for who in "-user automation@pve" "-token automation@pve!ansible"; do
  pveum aclmod /pool/homelab-svc     $who -role PVEVMAdmin
  pveum aclmod /storage/nvme0pool    $who -role PVEDatastoreUser
  pveum aclmod /storage/local        $who -role PVEDatastoreUser
done

# 2. secrets
cp inventory/group_vars/all_vault.yml.example inventory/group_vars/all_vault.yml
$EDITOR inventory/group_vars/all_vault.yml      # token secret + Mullvad fields
ansible-vault encrypt inventory/group_vars/all_vault.yml

# 3. your public SSH key
$EDITOR inventory/group_vars/all.yml            # set admin_ssh_pubkey

# 4. confirm you can reach PVE root over SSH (image fetch/snippet render use it)
ssh root@192.168.1.10 true
```

## Deploy

```bash
ansible-playbook site.yml --ask-vault-pass
```

Or via the Makefile (see `make help` for all targets):

```bash
make deps          # one-time: collections + python libs
make preflight     # syntax + inventory + ping, before a real run
make deploy        # full unattended deploy + ntfy
make check         # dry-run with diff (drift detection)
make verify        # gate assertions only + ntfy
make dl            # just svc-download   (ARGS="--tags jail" etc.)
make media         # just svc-media
make access        # (re)run tailscale + caddy on svc-media, e.g. after adding the authkey
make reconcile     # push cores/memory/onboot/startup onto EXISTING VMs (opt-in drift repair)
make vault-edit    # edit the encrypted secrets
```

Every target prompts for the vault password once. To run non-interactively
(required for the nightly timer): `echo 'pass' > .vault_pass && chmod 600
.vault_pass`, then add `USE_VAULT_FILE=1` to any target. `.vault_pass` is
gitignored. Pass extra ansible flags with `ARGS=`, e.g.
`make deploy ARGS="--limit svc-download --tags jail,verify"`.

Provisions both VMs, waits for cloud-init, builds the jail (gates block real
traffic until leak tests pass), starts the media stack, and pushes a green or
red ntfy at the end. Walk away; the phone tells you.

## Nightly health check (systemd timer)

`contrib/` ships a timer that runs the gate assertions nightly and only pings
you on failure — the green "all OK" ntfy is suppressed so a passing night is
silent. It also skips the fail-closed KILL test (`--skip-tags killtest`) so it
never interrupts in-flight downloads; run that one manually with
`make verify` when you want the full check.

Install on an always-on box that has the repo + vault password (your MBP or a
small LAN host — **not** the PVE host):

```bash
sudo cp -r homelab-iac /opt/homelab-iac        # or clone there
cd /opt/homelab-iac
echo 'YOUR_VAULT_PASS' > .vault_pass && chmod 600 .vault_pass
sudo cp contrib/systemd/homelab-verify.* /etc/systemd/system/
# edit User= and paths in the .service to match your box
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable --now homelab-verify.timer
systemctl list-timers homelab-verify.timer     # confirm next run
```

A failing gate fires an urgent ntfy (from the playbook's rescue block) and
marks the unit failed, so `systemctl --failed` catches it too. Test the wiring
any time with `sudo systemctl start homelab-verify.service` then
`journalctl -u homelab-verify.service -e`.

## Slices & drift

```bash
# resume just the jail on svc-download
ansible-playbook site.yml --limit svc-download --tags jail,verify --ask-vault-pass

# what would change? (day-two drift — IN-GUEST config only; VM shape is
# create-once and gets reconciled explicitly with `make reconcile`)
ansible-playbook site.yml --check --diff --ask-vault-pass

# gates only, e.g. from cron on the MBP
ansible-playbook verify.yml --tags verify --ask-vault-pass

# gates only, skipping the disruptive fail-closed kill test
ansible-playbook verify.yml --tags verify --skip-tags killtest --ask-vault-pass
```

Tags on the svc_download role: `files secrets nfs jail containers backup
verify`, plus `killtest` on the one fail-closed test that stops/starts the
jail. svc_media adds `media access backup`. Tag-sliced runs work in v5 (the
includes are `always`-tagged); failures still alert because rescue blocks are
`always`-tagged too.

## Updates & image policy (v5)

- **Containers:** the inert auto-update labels are GONE (there was no
  `podman-auto-update.timer`, so they advertised an update story that never
  ran). RomM is pinned to the release verified against this layout
  (`4.9.2`); everything else runs `:latest` at deploy time — after burn-in,
  pin what you have:  `podman inspect --format '{{ '{{' }}.ImageDigest{{ '}}' }}' jellyfin`
  then set `Image=docker.io/jellyfin/jellyfin@sha256:...` in the quadlet.
  Updates are a deliberate act: bump, restart, check.
- **Rocky image:** pinned to a dated build with exact-filename checksum
  verification (bump procedure in `group_vars/all.yml`). `.latest.` pins are
  refused by an assertion.

## Leak canary operations (v5)

A LEAK stops the download stack and writes `/var/lib/leak-canary/tripped`;
every 15-min run keeps alerting until you investigate and clear it:

```bash
rm /var/lib/leak-canary/tripped
systemctl start dl-{sabnzbd,prowlarr,sonarr,radarr}
```

Planned maintenance on the stack? `systemctl stop leak-canary.timer` first,
`systemctl start leak-canary.timer` after, or it will ping you about the
stopped services (that's it doing its job).

## Gotchas carried over from the bundle

- **host-backstop.nft input chain is default-drop.** It is now TEMPLATED
  from `lan_cidr` (`roles/svc_download/templates/host-backstop.nft.j2`), and
  the role asserts the rendered file matches `lan_cidr` before enabling
  nftables, so Ansible's own SSH survives. Tightening = change `lan_cidr`
  in group_vars (kept at the full /24 deliberately). Recovery is still
  `qm terminal 131` → `nft flush ruleset`.
- **peer.conf is wg(8) syntax** — the template emits only Interface/Peer;
  Address is WG_ADDR in vpn-netns.env, DNS in /etc/netns/vpn/resolv.conf.
- **RomM needs TWO env files** before it starts — `romm.env` (app secrets)
  AND `romm-db.env` (MariaDB bootstrap only; v5 splits them so provider API
  creds never enter the DB container). Required keys are in the quadlet
  headers; the role fails loudly listing them if either file is missing.
- **First run, no ntfy yet?** Pushes fail with a visible WARNING (not
  silently as before). Point `ntfy_url` at `https://ntfy.sh/<long-random>`
  in group_vars if you want pings before Phase 9.
- **PVE self-signed cert**: if you haven't put a trusted cert on :8006, set
  `pve_validate_certs: false` (default in group_vars/pve.yml).

## What this doesn't give you that Terraform would

Declarative VM *state* with a plan/apply/destroy lifecycle and a state file.
For two VMs on one node that's not worth the ceremony — `proxmox_kvm` is
idempotent enough (won't recreate an existing VMID), and `--check` covers
drift on the config side. If you later want true declarative infra, the
`bpg/proxmox` provider is the maintained choice; this repo's roles would
still do the in-guest half.

---
# ============================================================================
# SECRETS. Copy to all_vault.yml and encrypt:
#     cp all_vault.yml.example all_vault.yml
#     $EDITOR all_vault.yml          # fill in real values
#     ansible-vault encrypt all_vault.yml
# Edit later with:  ansible-vault edit all_vault.yml
# all_vault.yml is .gitignored; only the .example (this file) is committed.
# ============================================================================

# --- Proxmox API token (Phase 0 on thurgadin) — SCOPED (v5) ---------------
# v4 granted PVEVMAdmin at / with privilege separation disabled. v5 scopes
# the token to a pool holding only these two VMs + the two storages it
# actually touches, and keeps privsep ON (ACLs go on the token itself).
# Run ON thurgadin, once:
#
#   pveum pool add homelab-svc --comment "svc-download + svc-media"
#   pveum user add automation@pve
#   pveum user token add automation@pve ansible          # privsep stays 1
#   # VM lifecycle inside the pool only:
#   pveum aclmod /pool/homelab-svc -user automation@pve -role PVEVMAdmin
#   pveum aclmod /pool/homelab-svc -token 'automation@pve!ansible' -role PVEVMAdmin
#   # Disk allocation on the VM storage + snippet/image storage:
#   pveum aclmod /storage/nvme0pool -user automation@pve -role PVEDatastoreUser
#   pveum aclmod /storage/nvme0pool -token 'automation@pve!ansible' -role PVEDatastoreUser
#   pveum aclmod /storage/local     -user automation@pve -role PVEDatastoreUser
#   pveum aclmod /storage/local     -token 'automation@pve!ansible' -role PVEDatastoreUser
#
# Honest scope note: the qm import / EFI-disk steps run as root over SSH
# anyway (module limitation), so this scoping protects against TOKEN leakage,
# not against compromise of the control node's SSH key. If the first create
# 403s on some edge permission, widen deliberately (add Sys.Audit on /nodes/
# thurgadin via a custom role) rather than reverting to PVEVMAdmin-at-/.
vault_pve_token_id:     "automation@pve!ansible"
vault_pve_token_secret: "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx"

# --- Mullvad WireGuard (from your generated config; wg(8) fields only) ---
vault_mullvad_privkey:    "REPLACE_base64_private_key"
vault_mullvad_peer_pubkey: "REPLACE_mullvad_server_public_key"
vault_mullvad_endpoint_ip: "185.x.x.x"      # NUMERIC endpoint IP
# Any port your generated config uses is fine now — v5 derives BOTH the
# nftables endpoint-IP set and the endpoint-PORT set from peer.conf, so the
# backstop no longer assumes 51820.
vault_mullvad_endpoint_port: 51820
vault_mullvad_wg_addr:    "10.65.x.x/32"     # your assigned tunnel Address

# --- Tailscale (svc-media) -------------------------------------------------
# Pre-auth key from https://login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/keys
#   Recommended: Reusable=NO, Ephemeral=NO, Pre-authorized=YES,
#   Tags: tag:svc-media (if you use ACL tags), short expiry — the key is
#   only needed once; the node keeps its own credentials afterwards.
# Leave "" to keep `tailscale up` a manual step (role skips with a note,
# and Caddy is skipped too since it binds to the tailscale address).
vault_tailscale_authkey: ""

# --- ntfy auth (only if your ntfy requires a token; else leave empty) ---
vault_ntfy_token: ""

---
# ============================================================================
# pve_vm — provision one Rocky 10 service VM via the Proxmox API.
# Create-once by design: an existing VMID is left alone on normal runs so a
# converge can never surprise-reconfigure a running VM. Day-two shape changes
# (cores/memory/onboot/startup) are pushed EXPLICITLY with
# `-e pve_reconcile=true` (make reconcile). Disk size / CPU type / bridge /
# cicustom changes remain manual by intent — those want a maintenance window.
#
# Runs against localhost (control node) but the image tasks need to land ON
# thurgadin's filesystem, so those delegate over SSH to the PVE host. That
# means this ONE role needs SSH-to-root@thurgadin in addition to the API
# token. If you'd rather keep zero SSH to PVE, pre-seed the image by hand
# once and set pve_image_present=true to skip the fetch block.
# ============================================================================

- name: Fetch + verify Rocky image on the PVE host
  when: not (pve_image_present | default(false))
  delegate_to: "{{ pve_ssh_host | default('root@192.168.1.10') }}"
  block:

    - name: Check whether a verified image already exists
      ansible.builtin.stat:
        path: "{{ pve_image_dir }}/{{ rocky_image_name }}.sha256"
      register: img_stamp

    - name: Refuse mutable image pins
      ansible.builtin.assert:
        that:
          - "'.latest.' not in rocky_image_name"
        fail_msg: >-
          rocky_image_name must be a DATED build (see the bump procedure in
          group_vars/all.yml). '.latest.' pins are mutable upstream and defeat
          checksum verification.

    - name: Download image + its per-file CHECKSUM (only if no verified copy)
      when: not img_stamp.stat.exists
      ansible.builtin.get_url:
        url: "{{ item.url }}"
        dest: "{{ item.dest }}"
        mode: "0644"
      loop:
        - { url: "{{ rocky_image_url }}",    dest: "{{ pve_image_dir }}/{{ rocky_image_name }}" }
        - { url: "{{ rocky_checksum_url }}", dest: "{{ pve_image_dir }}/{{ rocky_image_name }}.CHECKSUM" }

    # v5: exact-line match. Rocky's per-file CHECKSUM is BSD-style:
    #   SHA256 (Rocky-10-GenericCloud-Base-10.2-....qcow2) = <hash>
    # The v4 `grep -q "$got" CHECKSUM` matched the hash ANYWHERE in the
    # aggregate file — any artifact's hash would have passed. This parses the
    # hash for exactly our filename and compares. (Authenticity, not just
    # integrity: optionally gpg --verify {{ rocky_checksum_url }}.asc.)
    - name: Verify sha256 against the exact CHECKSUM line and stamp it
      when: not img_stamp.stat.exists
      ansible.builtin.shell:
        cmd: |
          set -euo pipefail
          cd "{{ pve_image_dir }}"
          img="{{ rocky_image_name }}"
          want=$(awk -v f="($img)" '$1=="SHA256" && $2==f {print $4}' "$img.CHECKSUM")
          [ -n "$want" ] || { echo "FATAL: no SHA256 line for $img in its CHECKSUM"; exit 1; }
          got=$(sha256sum "$img" | awk '{print $1}')
          [ "$got" = "$want" ] || { echo "CHECKSUM MISMATCH: got=$got want=$want"; exit 1; }
          echo "$got  {{ pve_image_dir }}/$img" > "$img.sha256"
        executable: /bin/bash
      changed_when: true

- name: "Render cloud-init snippet for {{ vm_name }} onto the PVE host"
  delegate_to: "{{ pve_ssh_host | default('root@192.168.1.10') }}"
  ansible.builtin.template:
    src: user-data.yaml.j2
    dest: "{{ pve_snippet_dir }}/{{ vm_name }}-user.yaml"
    mode: "0644"

# Does the VM already exist? If so, creation is skipped (create-once).
- name: "Check whether VM {{ vm_id }} already exists"
  community.proxmox.proxmox_kvm:
    api_host:         "{{ pve_api_host }}"
    api_user:         "{{ vault_pve_token_id.split('!')[0] }}"
    api_token_id:     "{{ vault_pve_token_id.split('!')[1] }}"
    api_token_secret: "{{ vault_pve_token_secret }}"
    validate_certs:   "{{ pve_validate_certs | default(false) }}"
    node:             "{{ pve_node }}"
    vmid:             "{{ vm_id }}"
    state:            current
  register: vm_current
  failed_when: false
  changed_when: false

# Create the shell of the VM (everything declarative EXCEPT the imported boot
# disk + EFI disk, which the module handles poorly across 5.x builds). Those
# two run via `qm` over SSH — the reliable path.
- name: "Create VM {{ vm_id }} ({{ vm_name }}) — declarative config"
  when: vm_current.status is not defined
  community.proxmox.proxmox_kvm:
    api_host:         "{{ pve_api_host }}"
    api_user:         "{{ vault_pve_token_id.split('!')[0] }}"
    api_token_id:     "{{ vault_pve_token_id.split('!')[1] }}"
    api_token_secret: "{{ vault_pve_token_secret }}"
    validate_certs:   "{{ pve_validate_certs | default(false) }}"
    node:             "{{ pve_node }}"
    vmid:             "{{ vm_id }}"
    name:             "{{ vm_name }}"
    pool:             "{{ pve_vm_pool | default(omit) }}"
    ostype:           l26
    machine:          q35
    bios:             ovmf
    cpu:              "{{ pve_cpu_type }}"
    cores:            "{{ vm_cores }}"
    memory:           "{{ vm_memory }}"
    balloon:          0
    scsihw:           virtio-scsi-single
    agent:            "enabled=1,fstrim_cloned_disks=1"
    serial0:          socket
    vga:              serial0
    net:
      net0: "virtio,bridge={{ pve_bridge }}"
    ide:
      ide2: "{{ pve_storage }}:cloudinit"
    ciuser:           "{{ admin_user }}"
    nameservers:      "{{ lan_dns }}"
    searchdomains:    "{{ search_domain }}"
    ipconfig:
      ipconfig0: "ip={{ vm_ip }}/24,gw={{ lan_gateway }}"
    cicustom:         "user={{ pve_snippet_storage }}:snippets/{{ vm_name }}-user.yaml"
    onboot:           true
    startup:          "{{ vm_startup }}"
    state:            present
  register: vm_created

# v5: explicit day-two reconcile of the cheap-to-change knobs. Opt-in only.
# NOTE: on a RUNNING VM, cores/memory land as *pending* config and apply at
# the next guest power-cycle (balloon=0, no hotplug configured) — the task
# reports changed; `qm pending {{ vm_id }}` on thurgadin shows what's queued.
- name: "Reconcile VM {{ vm_id }} shape from host_vars (opt-in)"
  when:
    - vm_current.status is defined
    - pve_reconcile | default(false) | bool
  community.proxmox.proxmox_kvm:
    api_host:         "{{ pve_api_host }}"
    api_user:         "{{ vault_pve_token_id.split('!')[0] }}"
    api_token_id:     "{{ vault_pve_token_id.split('!')[1] }}"
    api_token_secret: "{{ vault_pve_token_secret }}"
    validate_certs:   "{{ pve_validate_certs | default(false) }}"
    node:             "{{ pve_node }}"
    vmid:             "{{ vm_id }}"
    name:             "{{ vm_name }}"
    cores:            "{{ vm_cores }}"
    memory:           "{{ vm_memory }}"
    onboot:           true
    startup:          "{{ vm_startup }}"
    update:           true

- name: "Attach EFI disk, import boot disk, size + boot order (qm, first create only)"
  when: vm_current.status is not defined
  delegate_to: "{{ pve_ssh_host | default('root@192.168.1.10') }}"
  ansible.builtin.shell:
    cmd: |
      set -euo pipefail
      # EFI vars disk; pre-enrolled-keys=0 => Secure Boot off
      qm set {{ vm_id }} --efidisk0 {{ pve_storage }}:1,efitype=4m,pre-enrolled-keys=0
      # Import the verified cloud image as scsi0, discard + iothread
      qm set {{ vm_id }} --scsi0 {{ pve_storage }}:0,import-from={{ pve_image_dir }}/{{ rocky_image_name }},discard=on,iothread=1
      qm set {{ vm_id }} --boot order=scsi0
      qm disk resize {{ vm_id }} scsi0 {{ vm_disk_gb }}G
    executable: /bin/bash
  changed_when: true

- name: "Start VM {{ vm_id }}"
  community.proxmox.proxmox_kvm:
    api_host:         "{{ pve_api_host }}"
    api_user:         "{{ vault_pve_token_id.split('!')[0] }}"
    api_token_id:     "{{ vault_pve_token_id.split('!')[1] }}"
    api_token_secret: "{{ vault_pve_token_secret }}"
    validate_certs:   "{{ pve_validate_certs | default(false) }}"
    node:             "{{ pve_node }}"
    vmid:             "{{ vm_id }}"
    state:            started

- name: "Wait for {{ vm_name }} SSH to answer (cloud-init still running)"
  ansible.builtin.wait_for:
    host: "{{ vm_ip }}"
    port: 22
    delay: 15
    timeout: 300

lines: +    # community.proxmox >= 1.x takes serial ports as a dict, not serial0=

content.text:
The deploy is running again in the background. Status of this debugging session so far — three compatibility/config bugs found and fixed, each one letting the play get further:

1. **ansible.cfg**: the `yaml` stdout callback was removed from community.general v12 — switched to the built-in equivalent (`stdout_callback = default` + `callback_result_format = yaml`)
2. **Vault never loaded** (latent repo bug): `group_vars/all_vault.yml` maps to a nonexistent group named `all_vault`, so its variables were silently ignored. Moved to the canonical layout — `group_vars/all/main.yml` + `group_vars/all/vault.yml` — and updated the README, Makefile, `.gitignore`, and the example file's instructions to match
3. **pve_vm role**: newer `community.proxmox` renamed the `serial0:` module parameter to a `serial:` dict

Also fixed along the way: added thurgadin's host key under its IP (`192.168.1.10`) to your known_hosts after verifying it matches the key you already trust for the hostname.

The play last got as far as image verification and cloud-init snippet rendering (all OK) before the serial-param failure — so the API token and its scoped ACLs are proven working. I'll report when this run finishes or hits the next snag, then commit all these fixes to GitHub once the deploy is green.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
# Physical/logical hosts. The two service VMs won't answer SSH until pve_vm
# has created them — that's why site.yml provisions before configuring, and
# why plays that touch them use `gather_facts` lazily.
all:
  children:
    # Runs API calls against PVE. These plays target localhost (the control
    # node / your MBP) and talk to the PVE API over HTTPS — we do NOT need
    # SSH-as-root to thurgadin for VM creation, only the API token.
    pve:
      hosts:
        thurgadin:
          ansible_connection: local   # API-driven; see group_vars/pve.yml
          pve_api_host: 192.168.1.10
          pve_node: thurgadin

    # In-guest configuration targets. SSH as straderb, become root.
    service_vms:
      hosts:
        svc-download:
          ansible_host: 192.168.1.31
        svc-media:
          ansible_host: 192.168.1.30
      vars:
        ansible_user: straderb
        ansible_become: true
        ansible_become_method: sudo

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# homelab-iac

Ansible-driven, git-versioned deployment of the netns-VPN-jail homelab
(thurgadin / convoker / svc-download / svc-media). This is the automation
layer over the `homelab-vpn-bundle` design — same architecture, same gates,
now runnable as one command with a check-back-later ntfy ping.

Run everything from the MBP. It talks to the PVE **API** (token) for VM
lifecycle and **SSH** for in-guest config. cloud-init stays minimal (identity
+ packages); Ansible owns everything after first boot, so day-two changes and
drift checks use the same code.

## What is and isn't automated

| Phase (DEPLOY.md)            | Here?  | Why |
|------------------------------|--------|-----|
| 1 TrueNAS storage            | **No** | One-time; the encryption reboot test you *want* to witness. Do it by hand. |
| 2 PVE host prep (RAM, order) | Partly | Snippet content + boot order are one-liners in DEPLOY; do those once. |
| 3/5 VM creation              | Yes    | `pve_vm` role via the API |
| 4 Jail + stack               | Yes    | `svc_download` role, gates as assertions |
| 6 Media stack                | Yes    | `svc_media` role, incl. Tailscale + Caddy **when `vault_tailscale_authkey` is set** (skipped with a visible note otherwise) |
| 7 App wiring                 | No     | Clicking through SAB/Prowlarr/arr/Jellyseerr UIs |
| 9 Backups                    | Yes    | Nightly timers on both VMs: RomM DB dump + appdata tars (media, 03:05), arr/SAB appdata tars (download, 03:10), 14-day retention. ABS uses its built-in backup to the NFS-mounted `/config/backups` — schedule it once in the UI. **Restore is manual; test it quarterly.** |
| 9 ntfy server                | No     | Stand it up on svc-media once; until then every push prints a loud WARNING in play output (or point `ntfy_url` at ntfy.sh/<random-topic> to bootstrap) |

Mullvad key generation is manual by nature.

## One-time setup

```bash
# 0. control node deps (MBP) — a project venv, because Homebrew's python
#    refuses global pip installs (PEP 668). The Makefile auto-detects .venv.
python3 -m venv .venv
.venv/bin/pip install ansible "proxmoxer>=2.0" requests
.venv/bin/ansible-galaxy collection install -r requirements.yml

# 1. PVE API token (on thurgadin, once) — SCOPED in v5: pool + two storages,
#    privilege separation stays ON. Full command block with rationale lives in
#    inventory/group_vars/all_vault.yml.example; short version:
pveum pool add homelab-svc --comment "svc-download + svc-media"
pveum user add automation@pve
pveum user token add automation@pve ansible                # copy the secret
set +H   # MUST be its own line first: interactive bash history expansion
         # mangles the !ansible token id ("event not found") otherwise
for who in "-user automation@pve" "-token automation@pve!ansible"; do
  pveum aclmod /pool/homelab-svc     $who -role PVEVMAdmin
  pveum aclmod /storage/nvme0pool    $who -role PVEDatastoreUser
  pveum aclmod /storage/local        $who -role PVEDatastoreUser
done

# 2. secrets — MUST live inside group_vars/all/ (a file named all_vault.yml
#    maps to a group called "all_vault", which doesn't exist, and is silently
#    never loaded)
cp inventory/group_vars/all_vault.yml.example inventory/group_vars/all/vault.yml
$EDITOR inventory/group_vars/all/vault.yml      # token secret + Mullvad fields
.venv/bin/ansible-vault encrypt inventory/group_vars/all/vault.yml

# 3. your public SSH key
$EDITOR inventory/group_vars/all/main.yml       # set admin_ssh_pubkey

# 4. confirm you can reach PVE root over SSH (image fetch/snippet render use it)
ssh root@192.168.1.10 true
```

## Deploy

```bash
ansible-playbook site.yml --ask-vault-pass
```

Or via the Makefile (see `make help` for all targets):

```bash
make deps          # one-time: collections + python libs
make preflight     # syntax + inventory + ping, before a real run
make deploy        # full unattended deploy + ntfy
make check         # dry-run with diff (drift detection)
make verify        # gate assertions only + ntfy
make dl            # just svc-download   (ARGS="--tags jail" etc.)
make media         # just svc-media
make access        # (re)run tailscale + caddy on svc-media, e.g. after adding the authkey
make reconcile     # push cores/memory/onboot/startup onto EXISTING VMs (opt-in drift repair)
make vault-edit    # edit the encrypted secrets
```

Every target prompts for the vault password once. To run non-interactively
(required for the nightly timer): `echo 'pass' > .vault_pass && chmod 600
.vault_pass`, then add `USE_VAULT_FILE=1` to any target. `.vault_pass` is
gitignored. Pass extra ansible flags with `ARGS=`, e.g.
`make deploy ARGS="--limit svc-download --tags jail,verify"`.

Provisions both VMs, waits for cloud-init, builds the jail (gates block real
traffic until leak tests pass), starts the media stack, and pushes a green or
red ntfy at the end. Walk away; the phone tells you.

## Nightly health check (systemd timer)

`contrib/` ships a timer that runs the gate assertions nightly and only pings
you on failure — the green "all OK" ntfy is suppressed so a passing night is
silent. It also skips the fail-closed KILL test (`--skip-tags killtest`) so it
never interrupts in-flight downloads; run that one manually with
`make verify` when you want the full check.

Install on an always-on box that has the repo + vault password (your MBP or a
small LAN host — **not** the PVE host):

```bash
sudo cp -r homelab-iac /opt/homelab-iac        # or clone there
cd /opt/homelab-iac
echo 'YOUR_VAULT_PASS' > .vault_pass && chmod 600 .vault_pass
sudo cp contrib/systemd/homelab-verify.* /etc/systemd/system/
# edit User= and paths in the .service to match your box
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable --now homelab-verify.timer
systemctl list-timers homelab-verify.timer     # confirm next run
```

A failing gate fires an urgent ntfy (from the playbook's rescue block) and
marks the unit failed, so `systemctl --failed` catches it too. Test the wiring
any time with `sudo systemctl start homelab-verify.service` then
`journalctl -u homelab-verify.service -e`.

## Slices & drift

```bash
# resume just the jail on svc-download
ansible-playbook site.yml --limit svc-download --tags jail,verify --ask-vault-pass

# what would change? (day-two drift — IN-GUEST config only; VM shape is
# create-once and gets reconciled explicitly with `make reconcile`)
ansible-playbook site.yml --check --diff --ask-vault-pass

# gates only, e.g. from cron on the MBP
ansible-playbook verify.yml --tags verify --ask-vault-pass

# gates only, skipping the disruptive fail-closed kill test
ansible-playbook verify.yml --tags verify --skip-tags killtest --ask-vault-pass
```

Tags on the svc_download role: `files secrets nfs jail containers backup
verify`, plus `killtest` on the one fail-closed test that stops/starts the
jail. svc_media adds `media access backup`. Tag-sliced runs work in v5 (the
includes are `always`-tagged); failures still alert because rescue blocks are
`always`-tagged too.

## Updates & image policy (v5)

- **Containers:** the inert auto-update labels are GONE (there was no
  `podman-auto-update.timer`, so they advertised an update story that never
  ran). RomM is pinned to the release verified against this layout
  (`4.9.2`); everything else runs `:latest` at deploy time — after burn-in,
  pin what you have:  `podman inspect --format '{{ '{{' }}.ImageDigest{{ '}}' }}' jellyfin`
  then set `Image=docker.io/jellyfin/jellyfin@sha256:...` in the quadlet.
  Updates are a deliberate act: bump, restart, check.
- **Rocky image:** pinned to a dated build with exact-filename checksum
  verification (bump procedure in `group_vars/all.yml`). `.latest.` pins are
  refused by an assertion.

## Leak canary operations (v5)

A LEAK stops the download stack and writes `/var/lib/leak-canary/tripped`;
every 15-min run keeps alerting until you investigate and clear it:

```bash
rm /var/lib/leak-canary/tripped
systemctl start dl-{sabnzbd,prowlarr,sonarr,radarr}
```

Planned maintenance on the stack? `systemctl stop leak-canary.timer` first,
`systemctl start leak-canary.timer` after, or it will ping you about the
stopped services (that's it doing its job).

## Gotchas carried over from the bundle

- **host-backstop.nft input chain is default-drop.** It is now TEMPLATED
  from `lan_cidr` (`roles/svc_download/templates/host-backstop.nft.j2`), and
  the role asserts the rendered file matches `lan_cidr` before enabling
  nftables, so Ansible's own SSH survives. Tightening = change `lan_cidr`
  in group_vars (kept at the full /24 deliberately). Recovery is still
  `qm terminal 131` → `nft flush ruleset`.
- **peer.conf is wg(8) syntax** — the template emits only Interface/Peer;
  Address is WG_ADDR in vpn-netns.env, DNS in /etc/netns/vpn/resolv.conf.
- **RomM needs TWO env files** before it starts — `romm.env` (app secrets)
  AND `romm-db.env` (MariaDB bootstrap only; v5 splits them so provider API
  creds never enter the DB container). Required keys are in the quadlet
  headers; the role fails loudly listing them if either file is missing.
- **First run, no ntfy yet?** Pushes fail with a visible WARNING (not
  silently as before). Point `ntfy_url` at `https://ntfy.sh/<long-random>`
  in group_vars if you want pings before Phase 9.
- **PVE self-signed cert**: if you haven't put a trusted cert on :8006, set
  `pve_validate_certs: false` (default in group_vars/pve.yml).

## What this doesn't give you that Terraform would

Declarative VM *state* with a plan/apply/destroy lifecycle and a state file.
For two VMs on one node that's not worth the ceremony — `proxmox_kvm` is
idempotent enough (won't recreate an existing VMID), and `--check` covers
drift on the config side. If you later want true declarative infra, the
`bpg/proxmox` provider is the maintained choice; this repo's roles would
still do the in-guest half.

#   pveum aclmod /storage/local     -token 'automation@pve!ansible' -role PVEDatastoreUser
#   # serial-port config is gated at / with Sys.Modify (PVE quirk); grant it
#   # via a single-privilege custom role, NOT PVEVMAdmin-at-/:
#   pveum role add homelab-sysmodify -privs Sys.Modify
#   pveum aclmod / -user automation@pve -role homelab-sysmodify
#   pveum aclmod / -token 'automation@pve!ansible' -role homelab-sysmodify

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
# ============================================================================
# SECRETS. Copy to all/vault.yml (NOT group_vars/all_vault.yml — that name
# maps to a nonexistent group and is silently ignored) and encrypt:
#     cp all_vault.yml.example all/vault.yml
#     $EDITOR all/vault.yml          # fill in real values
#     ansible-vault encrypt all/vault.yml
# Edit later with:  make vault-edit
# all/vault.yml is .gitignored; only the .example (this file) is committed.
# ============================================================================

# --- Proxmox API token (Phase 0 on thurgadin) — SCOPED (v5) ---------------
# v4 granted PVEVMAdmin at / with privilege separation disabled. v5 scopes
# the token to a pool holding only these two VMs + the two storages it
# actually touches, and keeps privsep ON (ACLs go on the token itself).
# Run ON thurgadin, once:
#
#   pveum pool add homelab-svc --comment "svc-download + svc-media"
#   pveum user add automation@pve
#   pveum user token add automation@pve ansible          # privsep stays 1
#   # VM lifecycle inside the pool only:
#   pveum aclmod /pool/homelab-svc -user automation@pve -role PVEVMAdmin
#   pveum aclmod /pool/homelab-svc -token 'automation@pve!ansible' -role PVEVMAdmin
#   # Disk allocation on the VM storage + snippet/image storage:
#   pveum aclmod /storage/nvme0pool -user automation@pve -role PVEDatastoreUser
#   pveum aclmod /storage/nvme0pool -token 'automation@pve!ansible' -role PVEDatastoreUser
#   pveum aclmod /storage/local     -user automation@pve -role PVEDatastoreUser
#   pveum aclmod /storage/local     -token 'automation@pve!ansible' -role PVEDatastoreUser
#
# Honest scope note: the qm import / EFI-disk steps run as root over SSH
# anyway (module limitation), so this scoping protects against TOKEN leakage,
# not against compromise of the control node's SSH key. If the first create
# 403s on some edge permission, widen deliberately (add Sys.Audit on /nodes/
# thurgadin via a custom role) rather than reverting to PVEVMAdmin-at-/.
vault_pve_token_id:     "automation@pve!ansible"
vault_pve_token_secret: "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx"

# --- Mullvad WireGuard (from your generated config; wg(8) fields only) ---
vault_mullvad_privkey:    "REPLACE_base64_private_key"
vault_mullvad_peer_pubkey: "REPLACE_mullvad_server_public_key"
vault_mullvad_endpoint_ip: "185.x.x.x"      # NUMERIC endpoint IP
# Any port your generated config uses is fine now — v5 derives BOTH the
# nftables endpoint-IP set and the endpoint-PORT set from peer.conf, so the
# backstop no longer assumes 51820.
vault_mullvad_endpoint_port: 51820
vault_mullvad_wg_addr:    "10.65.x.x/32"     # your assigned tunnel Address

# --- Tailscale (svc-media) -------------------------------------------------
# Pre-auth key from https://login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/keys
#   Recommended: Reusable=NO, Ephemeral=NO, Pre-authorized=YES,
#   Tags: tag:svc-media (if you use ACL tags), short expiry — the key is
#   only needed once; the node keeps its own credentials afterwards.
# Leave "" to keep `tailscale up` a manual step (role skips with a note,
# and Caddy is skipped too since it binds to the tailscale address).
vault_tailscale_authkey: ""

# --- ntfy auth (only if your ntfy requires a token; else leave empty) ---
vault_ntfy_token: ""

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
# ============================================================================
# SECRETS. Copy to all/vault.yml (NOT group_vars/all_vault.yml — that name
# maps to a nonexistent group and is silently ignored) and encrypt:
#     cp all_vault.yml.example all/vault.yml
#     $EDITOR all/vault.yml          # fill in real values
#     ansible-vault encrypt all/vault.yml
# Edit later with:  make vault-edit
# all/vault.yml is .gitignored; only the .example (this file) is committed.
# ============================================================================

# --- Proxmox API token (Phase 0 on thurgadin) — SCOPED (v5) ---------------
# v4 granted PVEVMAdmin at / with privilege separation disabled. v5 scopes
# the token to a pool holding only these two VMs + the two storages it
# actually touches, and keeps privsep ON (ACLs go on the token itself).
# Run ON thurgadin, once:
#
#   pveum pool add homelab-svc --comment "svc-download + svc-media"
#   pveum user add automation@pve
#   pveum user token add automation@pve ansible          # privsep stays 1
#   # VM lifecycle inside the pool only:
#   pveum aclmod /pool/homelab-svc -user automation@pve -role PVEVMAdmin
#   pveum aclmod /pool/homelab-svc -token 'automation@pve!ansible' -role PVEVMAdmin
#   # Disk allocation on the VM storage + snippet/image storage:
#   pveum aclmod /storage/nvme0pool -user automation@pve -role PVEDatastoreUser
#   pveum aclmod /storage/nvme0pool -token 'automation@pve!ansible' -role PVEDatastoreUser
#   pveum aclmod /storage/local     -user automation@pve -role PVEDatastoreUser
#   pveum aclmod /storage/local     -token 'automation@pve!ansible' -role PVEDatastoreUser
#   # serial-port config is gated at / with Sys.Modify (PVE quirk); grant it
#   # via a single-privilege custom role, NOT PVEVMAdmin-at-/:
#   pveum role add homelab-sysmodify -privs Sys.Modify
#   pveum aclmod / -user automation@pve -role homelab-sysmodify
#   pveum aclmod / -token 'automation@pve!ansible' -role homelab-sysmodify
#
# Honest scope note: the qm import / EFI-disk steps run as root over SSH
# anyway (module limitation), so this scoping protects against TOKEN leakage,
# not against compromise of the control node's SSH key. If the first create
# 403s on some edge permission, widen deliberately (add Sys.Audit on /nodes/
# thurgadin via a custom role) rather than reverting to PVEVMAdmin-at-/.
vault_pve_token_id:     "automation@pve!ansible"
vault_pve_token_secret: "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx"

# --- Mullvad WireGuard (from your generated config; wg(8) fields only) ---
vault_mullvad_privkey:    "REPLACE_base64_private_key"
vault_mullvad_peer_pubkey: "REPLACE_mullvad_server_public_key"
vault_mullvad_endpoint_ip: "185.x.x.x"      # NUMERIC endpoint IP
# Any port your generated config uses is fine now — v5 derives BOTH the
# nftables endpoint-IP set and the endpoint-PORT set from peer.conf, so the
# backstop no longer assumes 51820.
vault_mullvad_endpoint_port: 51820
vault_mullvad_wg_addr:    "10.65.x.x/32"     # your assigned tunnel Address

# --- Tailscale (svc-media) -------------------------------------------------
# Pre-auth key from https://login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/keys
#   Recommended: Reusable=NO, Ephemeral=NO, Pre-authorized=YES,
#   Tags: tag:svc-media (if you use ACL tags), short expiry — the key is
#   only needed once; the node keeps its own credentials afterwards.
# Leave "" to keep `tailscale up` a manual step (role skips with a note,
# and Caddy is skipped too since it binds to the tailscale address).
vault_tailscale_authkey: ""

# --- ntfy auth (only if your ntfy requires a token; else leave empty) ---
vault_ntfy_token: ""

- name: "Render cloud-init snippet for {{ vm_name }} onto the PVE host"
  delegate_to: "{{ pve_ssh_host | default('root@192.168.1.10') }}"
  ansible.builtin.template:
    src: user-data.yaml.j2
    dest: "{{ pve_snippet_dir }}/{{ vm_name }}-user.yaml"
    mode: "0644"

# Does the VM already exist? If so, creation is skipped (create-once).
- name: "Check whether VM {{ vm_id }} already exists"
  community.proxmox.proxmox_kvm:
    api_host:         "{{ pve_api_host }}"
    api_user:         "{{ vault_pve_token_id.split('!')[0] }}"
    api_token_id:     "{{ vault_pve_token_id.split('!')[1] }}"

    validate_certs:   "{{ pve_validate_certs | default(false) }}"
    node:             "{{ pve_node }}"
    vmid:             "{{ vm_id }}"
    state:            current
  register: vm_current
  failed_when: false
  changed_when: false

input.old_string: ## Gotchas carried over from the bundle
input.new_string:
## Gotchas carried over from the bundle

- **Adopting a bundle-era VM?** Add it to the pool first:
  `pveum pool modify homelab-svc -vms 131`. The token's visibility is
  pool-scoped, so an existing VM outside the pool is invisible to the
  existence check — the play then tries to create it and dies on
  "VM 131 already exists". (`pveum pool list` doesn't render members;
  verify with `pvesh get /pools/homelab-svc`.)

content.text:
Deploy is off and running with both VMs reachable now. Recap of this round: svc-media's brand-new host key was verified from two independent vantage points (PVE host + MBP, identical) before trusting it — the strict host-key checking in ansible.cfg is a feature worth keeping, it just needs this one-time step for freshly minted VMs.

Now comes the real work: in-guest configuration — the VPN jail with leak-test gates on svc-download, and the media stack (minus Tailscale/Caddy, skipped without an authkey) plus first-time package/container pulls on svc-media. This is the long phase; I'll report the outcome when it finishes.

content.text:
Fixed and relaunched. The role now creates `/etc/netns/vpn` before writing the resolver into it — a genuine ordering bug in the repo (it only ever worked on machines where the bundle-era scripts had already made that directory by hand, and your adopted VM evidently didn't have it).

Also noting for later: the ntfy warning in that run is expected — `ntfy_url` points at `http://192.168.1.30:8080`, and the ntfy server on svc-media isn't stood up yet (that's Phase 9, manual per the README). The play results stay authoritative; you just don't get phone pings yet.

I'll report when this run completes.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
# ============================================================================
# Environment facts (audited 2026-05-31; image pin refreshed 2026-07-15).
# Non-secret — safe in git. Secrets live in group_vars/all_vault.yml
# (ansible-vault encrypted).
# ============================================================================

# --- Network / identity ---
lan_gateway:     192.168.1.1
lan_dns:         192.168.1.1
lan_cidr:        192.168.1.0/24     # admin scope (deliberate: whole flat LAN)
search_domain:   fort.wow
admin_user:      straderb
svc_uid:         10001
svc_gid:         10001
timezone:        America/New_York

# --- Hosts ---
truenas_ip:      192.168.1.20
svc_media_ip:    192.168.1.30
svc_download_ip: 192.168.1.31

# --- TrueNAS NFS (created by hand in Phase 1 — NOT automated) ---
nfs_media_export:   "/mnt/sata_wd_14tb/media"
nfs_backups_export: "/mnt/sata_wd_14tb/backups"
nfs_opts: "hard,nofail,x-systemd.automount,x-systemd.mount-timeout=30"

# --- Proxmox storage (from /etc/pve/storage.cfg) ---
pve_storage:         nvme0pool           # VM disks
pve_snippet_storage: local
pve_snippet_dir:     /var/lib/vz/snippets
pve_image_dir:       /var/lib/vz/template/cache
pve_bridge:          vmbr0
# EL10 userspace is x86-64-v3; PVE default (v2-AES) won't boot it. 'host' on
# the 3700X (Zen 2) qualifies. Use x86-64-v3 instead only for live migration.
pve_cpu_type:        host

# --- Rocky 10 GenericCloud image — PINNED (v5) --------------------------
# Pin a DATED build, never the mutable `.latest` symlink: latest can change
# upstream while the local .sha256 stamp keeps an old copy, and the old v4
# check matched the hash against ANY line of the aggregate CHECKSUM file.
# v5 fetches the per-image BSD-style CHECKSUM and matches the exact filename.
#
# BUMP PROCEDURE (deliberate, not automatic):
#   1. Browse https://dl.rockylinux.org/pub/rocky/10/images/x86_64/
#   2. Update the three values below to the new dated build.
#   3. rm the old image + .sha256 stamp in {{ pve_image_dir }} on thurgadin.
#   4. Note: Rocky prunes dated images of the PREVIOUS minor when a new one
#      lands, so a stale pin eventually 404s — that is your cue to bump.
#   Optional authenticity (not just transfer integrity): also fetch
#   {{ rocky_checksum_url }}.asc and `gpg --verify` against the Rocky key.
rocky_image_name: "Rocky-10-GenericCloud-Base-10.2-20260525.0.x86_64.qcow2"
rocky_image_url:  "https://dl.rockylinux.org/pub/rocky/10/images/x86_64/Rocky-10-GenericCloud-Base-10.2-20260525.0.x86_64.qcow2"
rocky_checksum_url: "https://dl.rockylinux.org/pub/rocky/10/images/x86_64/Rocky-10-GenericCloud-Base-10.2-20260525.0.x86_64.qcow2.CHECKSUM"

# --- Admin SSH key injected into both VMs (public key CONTENT, not a path) ---
# Paste your MBP ed25519 public key here (safe — it's a public key):
admin_ssh_pubkey: "ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIMjZAEI+FfdW74JRrWjUbSK9HmF7AVHaa8qlYsN9YSLB straderb@Mac.fort.wow"

# --- Mullvad in-tunnel DNS (public constant, not a secret) ---
mullvad_dns: "10.64.0.1"

# --- ntfy: where deploy results are pushed. ------------------------------
# Default is the LAN-internal ntfy you'll stand up on svc-media (Phase 9).
# FIRST RUN: that ntfy does not exist yet. Either point this at
# https://ntfy.sh with a long random topic for bootstrap, or accept that
# the push fails — v5 prints a loud WARNING in the play output instead of
# swallowing it (v4 was silent). The svc-download leak canary alerts to
# this same target; the host backstop opens egress to it automatically
# when the ntfy host == svc_media_ip.
ntfy_url:   "http://192.168.1.30:8080"     # LAN-internal ntfy on svc-media
ntfy_topic: "homelab-deploy"

# --- svc-media access layer -----------------------------------------------
# caddy  = install Tailscale + Caddy and wire the ts.net vhosts, fully
#          unattended IF vault_tailscale_authkey is set (skipped with a
#          visible note when tailscaled isn't authenticated yet).
# manual = v4 behavior: role stops at "containers healthy on the LAN";

input.new_string:
- name: Create appdata dirs (10001) + local incomplete
  ansible.builtin.file:
    path: "{{ item }}"
    state: directory
    owner: "{{ svc_uid }}"
    group: "{{ svc_gid }}"
    mode: "0755"
  loop:
    - /srv/appdata/sabnzbd
    - /srv/appdata/prowlarr
    - /srv/appdata/sonarr
    - /srv/appdata/radarr
    - /var/lib/sabnzbd-incomplete
  tags: [files]

# Mountpoints only — no owner/group: once the NFS mount is live (fstab from a
# previous run), chown/chgrp here hits the server through root-squash and
# fails; server-side ownership is authoritative (the write-probe below is the
# real check).
- name: Create NFS mountpoints
  ansible.builtin.file:
    path: "{{ item }}"
    state: directory
  loop:
    - /srv/media

- NFS write-probe and cleanup both passed (the setpriv fixes hold)

content.text: Deploy rerunning with keepalives. To answer your question directly: the previous run *looked* alive (process existed) but was actually hung — the nftables backstop load had killed its own SSH session, the real work on the VM had completed, and the worker was waiting forever for a reply. I killed it, added `ServerAliveInterval` to ansible.cfg so that class of hang self-terminates in about a minute from now on, and relaunched. Since the backstop table is already loaded on the VM, this run should sail through that task and into the WireGuard netns + leak gates. Will report.

content.content:
roles/svc_download/tasks/main.yml:136:- name: Install patch-window helper
roles/svc_download/tasks/main.yml:138:    src: patch-window.nft
roles/svc_download/tasks/main.yml:139:    dest: /etc/nftables/patch-window.nft
PATCHFILE
#!/usr/sbin/nft -f
# patch-window.nft — TEMPORARILY open host egress for dnf/registry.
# Usage:
#   nft -f /etc/nftables/patch-window.nft     # open
#   <run: dnf update / podman auto-update>
#   nft -f /etc/nftables/host-backstop.nft    # close (reload strict baseline)
# Adds accept rules to the existing output chain; reloading host-backstop.nft
# removes them by rebuilding the table.
add rule inet host_backstop output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 udp dport 53 accept
add rule inet host_backstop output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 tcp dport 53 accept
add rule inet host_backstop output tcp dport 443 accept

- name: Start download containers

input.new_string:
# First deploy: podman pulls images in the HOST netns, which the backstop
# fences — only the running containers live in the vpn netns. Open the patch
# window just for missing images; the strict backstop always reloads after.
- name: Check which container images are present
  ansible.builtin.command:
    cmd: "podman image exists {{ item }}"
  loop:
    - lscr.io/linuxserver/sabnzbd:latest
    - lscr.io/linuxserver/prowlarr:latest
    - lscr.io/linuxserver/sonarr:latest
    - lscr.io/linuxserver/radarr:latest
  register: dl_images
  failed_when: false
  changed_when: false
  tags: [containers]

- name: Pre-pull missing images through the patch window
  when: dl_images.results | selectattr('rc', 'ne', 0) | list | length > 0
  block:
    - name: Open patch window
      ansible.builtin.command:
        cmd: nft -f /etc/nftables/patch-window.nft
      changed_when: true

    - name: Pull missing images
      ansible.builtin.command:
        cmd: "podman pull {{ item.item }}"
      loop: "{{ dl_images.results | selectattr('rc', 'ne', 0) | list }}"
      loop_control:
        label: "{{ item.item }}"
      changed_when: true
  always:
    - name: Close patch window (reload strict backstop)
      ansible.builtin.command:
        cmd: nft -f /etc/nftables/host-backstop.nft
      changed_when: true
  tags: [containers]

- name: Start download containers
  ansible.builtin.systemd:
    name: "dl-{{ item }}.service"
    state: started
  loop: [sabnzbd, prowlarr, sonarr, radarr]
  tags: [containers]

content.text: Deploy running with the new pre-pull flow: it detects missing images, opens the repo's own patch window (host DNS + 443 only), pulls, and reloads the strict backstop in an `always` block so the jail can never be left open even if a pull fails. Image pulls for four containers will take a few minutes. This is plausibly the run that goes green on svc-download — then the play moves to svc-media's stack for the first time, which will pull its own images (Jellyfin, RomM, etc.) on a much longer leash. Will report.

#!/usr/sbin/nft -f
# patch-window.nft — TEMPORARILY open host egress for dnf/registry.
# Usage:
#   nft -f /etc/nftables/patch-window.nft     # open
#   <run: dnf update / podman auto-update>
#   nft -f /etc/nftables/host-backstop.nft    # close (reload strict baseline)
# Adds accept rules to the existing output chain; reloading host-backstop.nft
# removes them by rebuilding the table.
add rule inet host_backstop output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 udp dport 53 accept
add rule inet host_backstop output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 tcp dport 53 accept
add rule inet host_backstop output tcp dport 443 accept

input.old_string:
# Adds accept rules to the existing output chain; reloading host-backstop.nft
# removes them by rebuilding the table.
add rule inet host_backstop output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 udp dport 53 accept
add rule inet host_backstop output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 tcp dport 53 accept
add rule inet host_backstop output tcp dport 443 accept
input.new_string:
# INSERTS accept rules at the TOP of the output chain — `add` would append
# them below the chain's final `log ... drop` rule where they never match.
# Reloading host-backstop.nft removes them by rebuilding the table.
insert rule inet host_backstop output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 udp dport 53 accept
insert rule inet host_backstop output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 tcp dport 53 accept
insert rule inet host_backstop output tcp dport 443 accept

git add -A && git status --porcelain | head -15 && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Fixes from first live deploy run (12 issues found end-to-end)

Control node / plumbing:
- ansible.cfg: yaml callback removed in community.general 12 -> builtin
  default callback with callback_result_format=yaml
- ansible.cfg: SSH keepalives so nft ruleset loads that sever the session
  fail fast instead of hanging the worker forever
- hosts.yml: pin ansible_python_interpreter to the venv python for the
  API-driven local connection (proxmoxer lives there)
- group_vars: all_vault.yml mapped to a nonexistent "all_vault" group and
  was silently never loaded; moved to group_vars/all/{main,vault}.yml

PVE / provisioning:
- pve_vm: community.proxmox renamed serial0= to a serial: dict
- README: document Sys.Modify-at-/ and SDN.Use-on-bridge grants (403s on
  serial-port config and NIC attach with the scoped token), pool adoption
  for bundle-era VMs, and the set +H history-expansion gotcha

In-guest roles:
- svc_download: create /etc/netns/vpn before writing resolv.conf into it
- svc_download: don't manage owner/group on NFS mountpoints (root-squash
  rejects chgrp once the mount is live); split from appdata dirs task
- both roles: runuser -u rejects '#uid' -> setpriv for NFS write probes,
  probe cleanup, and backup subtree creation
- svc_download: pre-pull quadlet images through the patch window (pulls
  happen in the host netns, which the backstop fences); always reload the
  strict backstop after
- group_vars: NFS exports live inside the tank dataset (tank/media,
  tank/backups)

Known remaining issue: patch-window.nft uses `add rule`, which appends
below the chain's final drop — the opened window has no effect and image
pulls still fail. Next step: `insert rule` at the top of the chain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push && git log --oneline -2

Everything's committed and pushed — enjoy the break. ☕

**Commit `14fced3`** on `fortwowlabs/homelab-ironwood`: all 12 fixes from this debugging session (callback, vault loading, venv interpreter, proxmox module params, PVE permission grants documented, netns dir, NFS paths/ownership/setpriv, patch-window pre-pull, SSH keepalives), 10 files changed.

**Where we're pausing:** the deploy reaches 43 tasks on svc-download — VMs provisioned, NFS proven, jail built, **both leak gates passing** — and fails only at container image pulls. The one known fix left (deliberately *not* applied, per your rejection): change `add rule` → `insert rule` in [patch-window.nft](roles/svc_download/files/patch-window.nft), because appended accepts land below the chain's final drop and never match. That's noted in the commit message and my memory, so whenever you're back, it's a one-word-times-three edit and one more `make deploy` — realistically the run that goes green on svc-download and starts svc-media's first stack build.

First, the thing I most want to double-check: my pre-pull block closes the patch window with a bare `nft -f host-backstop.nft` reload — I need to confirm that doesn't wipe the Mullvad endpoint sets that the refresh script populated (which would silently break the WireGuard handshake path):

#!/usr/sbin/nft -f
# /etc/nftables/host-backstop.nft   (PRIMARY second layer — pfSense-free variant)
# RENDERED BY ANSIBLE from roles/svc_download/templates/host-backstop.nft.j2
#
# With no pfSense egress rules, THIS is the backstop behind the netns jail.
# It constrains the VM's INIT namespace (the host itself) — the jail needs
# no help, since inside it non-Mullvad destinations are simply unroutable.
# This guards the host surface: a unit accidentally started outside the jail,
# a misconfiguration, casual malware on the VM.
#
# Scope discipline: OWN table, explicit `delete table` guard, NO
# `flush ruleset`. Reloading this file cannot clobber wg-quick, podman NAT,
# or anything else, and nothing clobbers it.
#
# Endpoint IPs AND ports are injected from /etc/nftables/mullvad-endpoints.nft
# (generated by refresh-mullvad-endpoints.sh from peer.conf), so a Mullvad
# server OR port swap does not strand the handshake. v4 hardcoded udp/51820.
#
# Honest limit: root on the VM can delete this table. It raises the bar
# against misconfiguration and casual malware; it is NOT a security boundary.
# The boundary is the jail.
#
# firewalld must be OFF on this VM (this file owns BOTH directions now).

define TRUENAS = {{ truenas_ip }}
define LAN_ADMIN = {{ lan_cidr }}   # deliberate: whole flat LAN (owner's call)

table inet host_backstop
delete table inet host_backstop

table inet host_backstop {
    # Populated by mullvad-endpoints.nft (included below). Declared here so
    # the ruleset loads even before the first refresh.
    set mullvad_wg {
        type ipv4_addr
        flags interval
    }
    set mullvad_ports {
        type inet_service
    }

    # ---- OUTBOUND: default-drop; Mullvad handshake + NFS + essentials -----

input.command: cat roles/svc_download/files/refresh-mullvad-endpoints.sh
input.description: Show Mullvad endpoint refresh script

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# refresh-mullvad-endpoints.sh — regenerate /etc/nftables/mullvad-endpoints.nft
# from the Endpoint(s) in the WireGuard config, then atomically reload BOTH
# sets (endpoint IPs and endpoint PORTS).
#
# Why: host-backstop.nft permits the WG handshake only to known Mullvad
# IP:port pairs. If you swap Mullvad servers or the generated config uses a
# non-51820 port (they can), this keeps the backstop in sync so the new
# handshake isn't dropped. Endpoints are numeric IPs by design — this is a
# parse, not a DNS lookup; safe to run even when egress is locked down.
set -euo pipefail

WG_CONF=${1:-/etc/wireguard/peer.conf}
OUT=/etc/nftables/mullvad-endpoints.nft
TMP=$(mktemp)

# Extract IPv4 Endpoint hosts and ports. Supports multiple [Peer] blocks.
mapfile -t ips   < <(grep -iE '^\s*Endpoint\s*=' "$WG_CONF" \
    | sed -E 's/.*=\s*([0-9.]+):[0-9]+.*/\1/' | sort -u)
mapfile -t ports < <(grep -iE '^\s*Endpoint\s*=' "$WG_CONF" \
    | sed -E 's/.*=\s*[0-9.]+:([0-9]+).*/\1/' | sort -u)

[[ ${#ips[@]} -gt 0 && ${#ports[@]} -gt 0 ]] \
    || { echo "FATAL: no numeric IP:port Endpoint found in $WG_CONF" >&2; exit 1; }

{
    echo "# generated $(date -Is) from $WG_CONF — do not edit by hand"
    echo "add element inet host_backstop mullvad_wg { $(IFS=,; echo "${ips[*]}") }"
    echo "add element inet host_backstop mullvad_ports { $(IFS=,; echo "${ports[*]}") }"
} > "$TMP"

# Load the table first if absent (idempotent), then flush+repopulate the sets.
nft list table inet host_backstop &>/dev/null || nft -f /etc/nftables/host-backstop.nft
nft flush set inet host_backstop mullvad_wg
nft flush set inet host_backstop mullvad_ports
install -m 644 "$TMP" "$OUT"
nft -f "$OUT"
rm -f "$TMP"
logger -t mullvad-endpoints "refreshed: ips=${ips[*]} ports=${ports[*]}"
echo "OK: ips=${ips[*]} ports=${ports[*]}"

log prefix "host-backstop-out-drop " counter drop
    }

    # ---- INBOUND: replaces firewalld on this VM ---------------------------
    chain input {
        type filter hook input priority 0; policy drop;

        iifname "lo" accept
        ct state established,related accept
        ct state invalid drop

        ip saddr $LAN_ADMIN tcp dport 22 accept   # SSH from admin

        # Jailed-service admin UIs via the veth socket proxies (proxies bind
        # these ports on the LAN IP). v5: 5076 gone with NZBHydra2.
        ip saddr $LAN_ADMIN tcp dport { 8080, 9696, 8989, 7878 } accept

        icmp type echo-request accept

        log prefix "host-backstop-in-drop " counter drop
    }

    # No forward chain: nothing routes THROUGH this VM's init ns.
}

include "/etc/nftables/mullvad-endpoints.nft"
2

input.content:
# Review of deploy session + plan to finish the deployment

## Context

First live deployment of homelab-iac (now `fortwowlabs/homelab-ironwood`) surfaced 12 issues, all fixed and pushed as `14fced3`. The deploy currently reaches 43/~70 tasks on svc-download — VMs provisioned, NFS proven, jail built, **both leak gates pass** — and stops at container image pulls. One known fix is designed but deliberately unapplied (user paused for a break mid-edit). This plan reviews the previous work and lays out the remaining steps to a green deploy.

## Review findings (read-only audit, this session)

**Verified correct by later runs** — each fix demonstrably unblocked the next stage:
- ansible.cfg callback + keepalives; venv interpreter pin ([inventory/hosts.yml](inventory/hosts.yml))
- `group_vars/all/` restructure (vault actually loads now)
- `serial:` dict for community.proxmox ([roles/pve_vm/tasks/main.yml](roles/pve_vm/tasks/main.yml))
- netns dir creation, NFS `tank/` paths, mountpoint-ownership split, setpriv probes ([roles/svc_download/tasks/main.yml](roles/svc_download/tasks/main.yml))
- Server-side: scoped `Sys.Modify`/`SDN.Use` custom roles, VM 131 pool adoption — all documented in README + vault example
- Secrets hygiene confirmed: plaintext vault + `.vault_pass` never entered either commit

**Newly verified this review:**
- Closing the patch window via bare `nft -f host-backstop.nft` reload is SAFE: the template ends with `include "/etc/nftables/mullvad-endpoints.nft"`, so the Mullvad endpoint sets repopulate on reload — no stranded-handshake risk in the pre-pull `always:` block.
- The pending `insert rule` fix is confirmed correct: the output chain ends with `log ... counter drop`, so `add rule` (append) is dead code; `insert` places accepts at the top.

**Minor debts noted (non-blocking):**
- Pre-pull image list in svc_download tasks is hardcoded, duplicating quadlet `Image=` lines — drift risk if images change.
- The three svc_media setpriv call-sites are the same pattern as the two proven svc_download ones but haven't executed yet (play never reached that role).
- `bundle-reference/svc-download/patch-window.nft` still has the broken `add rule` pattern (reference copy).

## Next known blocker (found during review)

When svc-download goes green, svc_media's role runs for the first time and **hard-fails by design** unless `/opt/homelab/appdata/romm/romm.env` + `romm-db.env` exist on the fresh VM ([roles/svc_media/tasks/main.yml:165-182](roles/svc_media/tasks/main.yml#L165-L182)). These carry user-authored secrets (DB passwords, `ROMM_AUTH_SECRET_KEY`, optional metadata-provider creds).

## Plan

1. **Apply the patch-window fix** in [roles/svc_download/files/patch-window.nft](roles/svc_download/files/patch-window.nft): three `add rule` → `insert rule`, update header comment. Mirror the same fix in `bundle-reference/svc-download/patch-window.nft` so nobody copy-pastes the broken pattern.
2. **Pre-seed the RomM env files** on svc-media (per answer to the open question below), so the run doesn't stop there.
3. **Rerun** `make deploy USE_VAULT_FILE=1` (background, log to scratchpad). Expected long run: svc-media pulls Jellyfin/RomM/ABS/Jellyseerr images (no backstop there — no patch window needed).
4. **Fix-forward any new svc-media failures** the same way as before (diagnose from log, patch role, rerun; Tailscale/Caddy skip with a note — no authkey — is expected and fine).
5. **On green:** run `make verify` (full gates incl. killtest, once, deliberately); spot-check SAB/Prowlarr/Sonarr/Radarr UIs via the socket proxies and Jellyfin on the LAN IP.
6. **Commit + push** all new fixes; update memory (`deploy-state.md`).

Optional cleanups if desired after green (small, separate commit): derive the pre-pull image list from the quadlet files.

## Verification

- The deploy log itself is the harness: PLAY RECAP with `failed=0` on all three hosts, leak gates green, ntfy WARNING (expected, no server yet).
- `make verify` passes end-to-end.
- Manual spot-check: `curl` the four proxied UI ports on 192.168.1.31 and Jellyfin `:8096` on 192.168.1.30.

options.label: Generate for me (Recommended)

# Review of deploy session + plan to finish the deployment

## Context

First live deployment of homelab-iac (now `fortwowlabs/homelab-ironwood`) surfaced 12 issues, all fixed and pushed as `14fced3`. The deploy currently reaches 43/~70 tasks on svc-download — VMs provisioned, NFS proven, jail built, **both leak gates pass** — and stops at container image pulls. One known fix is designed but deliberately unapplied (user paused for a break mid-edit). This plan reviews the previous work and lays out the remaining steps to a green deploy.

## Review findings (read-only audit, this session)

**Verified correct by later runs** — each fix demonstrably unblocked the next stage:
- ansible.cfg callback + keepalives; venv interpreter pin ([inventory/hosts.yml](inventory/hosts.yml))
- `group_vars/all/` restructure (vault actually loads now)
- `serial:` dict for community.proxmox ([roles/pve_vm/tasks/main.yml](roles/pve_vm/tasks/main.yml))
- netns dir creation, NFS `tank/` paths, mountpoint-ownership split, setpriv probes ([roles/svc_download/tasks/main.yml](roles/svc_download/tasks/main.yml))
- Server-side: scoped `Sys.Modify`/`SDN.Use` custom roles, VM 131 pool adoption — all documented in README + vault example
- Secrets hygiene confirmed: plaintext vault + `.vault_pass` never entered either commit

**Newly verified this review:**
- Closing the patch window via bare `nft -f host-backstop.nft` reload is SAFE: the template ends with `include "/etc/nftables/mullvad-endpoints.nft"`, so the Mullvad endpoint sets repopulate on reload — no stranded-handshake risk in the pre-pull `always:` block.
- The pending `insert rule` fix is confirmed correct: the output chain ends with `log ... counter drop`, so `add rule` (append) is dead code; `insert` places accepts at the top.

**Minor debts noted (non-blocking):**
- Pre-pull image list in svc_download tasks is hardcoded, duplicating quadlet `Image=` lines — drift risk if images change.
- The three svc_media setpriv call-sites are the same pattern as the two proven svc_download ones but haven't executed yet (play never reached that role).
- `bundle-reference/svc-download/patch-window.nft` still has the broken `add rule` pattern (reference copy).

## Next known blocker (found during review)

When svc-download goes green, svc_media's role runs for the first time and **hard-fails by design** unless `/opt/homelab/appdata/romm/romm.env` + `romm-db.env` exist on the fresh VM ([roles/svc_media/tasks/main.yml:165-182](roles/svc_media/tasks/main.yml#L165-L182)). These carry user-authored secrets (DB passwords, `ROMM_AUTH_SECRET_KEY`, optional metadata-provider creds).

## Plan

1. **Apply the patch-window fix** in [roles/svc_download/files/patch-window.nft](roles/svc_download/files/patch-window.nft): three `add rule` → `insert rule`, update header comment. Mirror the same fix in `bundle-reference/svc-download/patch-window.nft` so nobody copy-pastes the broken pattern.
2. **Pre-seed the RomM env files** on svc-media (per answer to the open question below), so the run doesn't stop there.
3. **Rerun** `make deploy USE_VAULT_FILE=1` (background, log to scratchpad). Expected long run: svc-media pulls Jellyfin/RomM/ABS/Jellyseerr images (no backstop there — no patch window needed).
4. **Fix-forward any new svc-media failures** the same way as before (diagnose from log, patch role, rerun; Tailscale/Caddy skip with a note — no authkey — is expected and fine).
5. **On green:** run `make verify` (full gates incl. killtest, once, deliberately); spot-check SAB/Prowlarr/Sonarr/Radarr UIs via the socket proxies and Jellyfin on the LAN IP.
6. **Commit + push** all new fixes; update memory (`deploy-state.md`).

Optional cleanups if desired after green (small, separate commit): derive the pre-pull image list from the quadlet files.

## Verification

- The deploy log itself is the harness: PLAY RECAP with `failed=0` on all three hosts, leak gates green, ntfy WARNING (expected, no server yet).
- `make verify` passes end-to-end.
- Manual spot-check: `curl` the four proxied UI ports on 192.168.1.31 and Jellyfin `:8096` on 192.168.1.30.

-2. **Pre-seed the RomM env files** on svc-media (per answer to the open question below), so the run doesn't stop there.

Both files 0600, owner 10001, in `/opt/homelab/appdata/romm/`. Secrets generated ON the VM (never pass through chat/logs).

#!/usr/sbin/nft -f
# patch-window.nft — TEMPORARILY open host egress for dnf/registry.
# Usage:
#   nft -f /etc/nftables/patch-window.nft     # open
#   <run: dnf update / podman auto-update>
#   nft -f /etc/nftables/host-backstop.nft    # close (reload strict baseline)
# Adds accept rules to the existing output chain; reloading host-backstop.nft
# removes them by rebuilding the table.
add rule inet host_backstop output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 udp dport 53 accept
add rule inet host_backstop output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 tcp dport 53 accept
add rule inet host_backstop output tcp dport 443 accept

toolUseResult.newString:
# INSERTS accept rules at the TOP of the output chain — `add` would append
# them below the chain's final `log ... drop` rule where they never match.
# Reloading host-backstop.nft removes them by rebuilding the table.
insert rule inet host_backstop output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 udp dport 53 accept
insert rule inet host_backstop output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 tcp dport 53 accept
insert rule inet host_backstop output tcp dport 443 accept
toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/sbin/nft -f
# patch-window.nft — TEMPORARILY open host egress for dnf/registry.
# Usage:
#   nft -f /etc/nftables/patch-window.nft     # open
#   <run: dnf update / podman auto-update>
#   nft -f /etc/nftables/host-backstop.nft    # close (reload strict baseline)
# Adds accept rules to the existing output chain; reloading host-backstop.nft
# removes them by rebuilding the table.
add rule inet host_backstop output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 udp dport 53 accept
add rule inet host_backstop output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 tcp dport 53 accept
add rule inet host_backstop output tcp dport 443 accept

+insert rule inet host_backstop output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 tcp dport 53 accept
+insert rule inet host_backstop output tcp dport 443 accept

# v5: image PINNED to the release verified against these mounts (matches the
# upstream 4.9.x quickstart exactly, embedded-Valkey layout incl. the
# /redis-data volume). Bump DELIBERATELY: check the release notes, update the
# tag, restart, confirm the migration in `podman logs romm`.
[Unit]
Description=RomM
Requires=romm-db.service
After=romm-db.service

[Container]
Image=docker.io/rommapp/romm:4.9.2
ContainerName=romm
Network=romm.network
# ~homelab/.config/containers/systemd/romm-db.container
#
# v5: DB gets its OWN env file (romm-db.env) so the app container's secrets
# (auth key, metadata-provider API creds) never enter the DB container.
# romm-db.env must define exactly:
#   MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD=...
#   MARIADB_DATABASE=romm
#   MARIADB_USER=romm-user
#   MARIADB_PASSWORD=...        # must match DB_PASSWD in romm.env
#
# Notify=healthy: the unit only reports started once the HealthCmd passes,
# so romm.service (Requires/After) waits for a READY database, not just a
# started container.
[Unit]
Description=RomM MariaDB

[Container]
Image=docker.io/library/mariadb:11

chmod 600 /opt/homelab/appdata/romm/romm.env /opt/homelab/appdata/romm/romm-db.env

input.old_string: - name: Enable + start UI socket proxies
input.new_string:
# The proxy sockets/services run as systemd_socket_proxyd_t, which SELinux
# confines to specific ports. Without these booleans the socket binds fail
# with EACCES and NO audit record (systemd's internal labeling path).
- name: Allow systemd-socket-proxyd any bind/connect (SELinux booleans)
  ansible.posix.seboolean:
    name: "{{ item }}"
    state: true
    persistent: true
  loop: [systemd_socket_proxyd_bind_any, systemd_socket_proxyd_connect_any]
  tags: [containers]

- name: Enable + start UI socket proxies

tags: [backup]

# ------------------------------------------------------------------ verify ---
# The gate that matters: every container is INSIDE the jail netns.
- name: "GATE: each dl-* container is in /run/netns/vpn"
  ansible.builtin.shell:
    cmd: |
      set -euo pipefail
      want=$(stat -Lc %i /run/netns/vpn)
      for c in sabnzbd prowlarr sonarr radarr; do
        key=$(podman inspect -f '{{ '{{' }}.NetworkSettings.SandboxKey{{ '}}' }}' "$c")
        [ "$(stat -Lc %i "$key")" = "$want" ] || { echo "LEAK: $c not in jail ($key)"; exit 1; }
      done
      echo "all-in-jail"
    executable: /bin/bash
  register: jail_membership
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: "'all-in-jail' not in jail_membership.stdout"
  tags: [verify]

- name: "GATE: Mullvad confirmed from inside a container"
  ansible.builtin.command:
    cmd: podman exec sabnzbd curl -4 -s --max-time 15 https://am.i.mullvad.net/connected

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# leak-canary.sh — continuous verification of the jail invariants.
# RENDERED BY ANSIBLE from roles/svc_download/templates/leak-canary.sh.j2.
# Run by leak-canary.timer every 15 min. Converts "we leak-tested it once in
# July" into an enforced property.
#
# v5 fixes over v4:
#   - Checks an EXPLICIT expected list, not `podman ps` output. v4 iterated
#     only RUNNING containers, so "everything stopped" reported OK — and
#     since fail() stops the stack, one leak produced one alert and then
#     green forever.
#   - A leak writes a persistent tripped flag ($FLAG); every
#     subsequent run keeps alerting until you clear it:
#         rm /var/lib/leak-canary/tripped && systemctl start dl-{sabnzbd,prowlarr,sonarr,radarr}
#   - Alerts actually fire (ntfy on the LAN — host-backstop opens that path).
#   - Planned maintenance: `systemctl stop leak-canary.timer` first, or the
#     "service not active" alert will ping you.
#
# Severity model:
#   LEAK  (wrong netns / foreign route / wrong egress IP)  -> stop stack,
#         write flag, urgent alert, exit 1.
#   DOWN  (expected container missing / unit inactive)     -> high alert,
#         exit 1, NO stop (nothing to protect) and NO flag.
#   A stalled egress curl (tunnel down) is NOT a failure — fail-closed working.
set -uo pipefail

STATE_DIR=/var/lib/leak-canary
FLAG="$STATE_DIR/tripped"
NTFY="{{ ntfy_url }}/{{ ntfy_topic }}"
expected=(sabnzbd prowlarr sonarr radarr)

mkdir -p "$STATE_DIR"

alert() {  # $1=priority $2=title $3=body — best effort, never gates logic
    logger -p auth.crit -t leak-canary "$2 — $3"
    curl -fsS --max-time 5 -X POST \
        -H "Title: $2" -H "Priority: $1" -H "Tags: rotating_light" \
{% if (vault_ntfy_token | default('')) | length > 0 %}
        -H "Authorization: Bearer {{ vault_ntfy_token }}" \
{% endif %}
        -d "$3" "$NTFY" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
        || logger -p auth.crit -t leak-canary "ALERT DELIVERY FAILED (ntfy unreachable): $2"
}

fail_leak() {
    date -Is > "$FLAG"
    systemctl stop 'dl-*.service' 2>/dev/null
    alert urgent "LEAK: download jail" "$1 — download stack STOPPED. Investigate, then: rm $FLAG && systemctl start dl-{sabnzbd,prowlarr,sonarr,radarr}"
    exit 1
}

down() {
    alert high "download stack degraded" "$1 (planned maintenance? stop leak-canary.timer to silence)"
    exit 1
}

# 0. A previously-tripped canary stays loud until a human clears it.
if [[ -f "$FLAG" ]]; then
    alert high "download stack HALTED (leak flag)" "tripped $(cat "$FLAG"); still halted. Investigate, then rm $FLAG and restart dl-* units."
    exit 1
fi

# 1. Every expected container must exist, its unit must be active, and it
#    must live in the jail. SandboxKey is the netns path Podman joined;
#    anything else means a unit drifted (e.g. someone removed PodmanArgs and
#    it silently fell back to the default bridge).
want_inode=$(stat -Lc %i /run/netns/vpn) || fail_leak "netns /run/netns/vpn missing"
for c in "${expected[@]}"; do
    podman container exists "$c" \
        || down "expected container $c does not exist"
    systemctl is-active --quiet "dl-$c.service" \
        || down "dl-$c.service is not active"
    key=$(podman inspect -f '{% raw %}{{.NetworkSettings.SandboxKey}}{% endraw %}' "$c")
    [[ -n "$key" ]] || fail_leak "container $c has no sandbox netns"
    [[ "$(stat -Lc %i "$key" 2>/dev/null)" == "$want_inode" ]] \
        || fail_leak "container $c is NOT in the vpn netns (sandbox: $key)"
done

# 2. Topology drift.
ip -n vpn route show default | grep -q 'dev wg0' \
    || fail_leak "default route in netns is no longer wg0"
ip -n vpn route show | grep -Ev 'dev (lo|wg0|veth-vpn)' | grep -q . \
    && fail_leak "unexpected route present in netns"

# 3. Egress identity. Timeout/stall = tunnel down = fail-closed = OK.
egress=$(ip netns exec vpn curl -4 -s --max-time 10 https://am.i.mullvad.net/connected || true)
if [[ -n "$egress" && "$egress" != *"You are connected to Mullvad"* ]]; then
    fail_leak "egress check says NOT Mullvad: $egress"
fi

logger -t leak-canary "OK (egress=${egress:-tunnel-down/fail-closed})"
exit 0
